manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: aircraft, statute, jukebox, toothpick, dime, income, ketchup, inmate, egghead.
Your opponent's team words are: commie, newcomer, cactus, ordinance, eggplant, soccer, concierge, hypocrite.
Distractor words are: nuisance, ozone, tequila, decorator, stewardess, motorcade, pond.
The kill words are: titanium.

message.reasoning:
The user asks to provide a single-word clue and list of related target words, in a specific format. Must be a clue that relates to some of team's words: aircraft, statute, jukebox, toothpick, dime, income, ketchup? Wait ketchup is not a team word? Actually team words list: aircraft, statute, jukebox, toothpick, dime, income, ketchup, inmate, egghead. Yes ketchup is there. The clue cannot be any of the words in the lists nor contain parts of them. Must be semantically related. Choose some set of target words that are related.

We need to think of a clue word: maybe "small" could relate to toothpick, dime, egghead? Not exactly. "Music" could relate to jukebox, maybe aircraft? Not.

Perhaps "legal" relates to statute and maybe income (tax). But maybe too ambiguous with ordinance (opponent). Must avoid clue that is part of opponent words.

Could clue "money"? Money relates to dime, income. Also maybe "statute" (tax law) but not sure. Money is not in any list. It's okay. But could also inadvertently link to "dime" and "income". That's two.

But we could try more: "breakfast"? Relates to egghead (egg) and maybe ketchup (like eggs and ketchup?) Not strong.

"Snack"? toothpick, ketchup, maybe.

"Flight"? aircraft, maybe inmate? No.

"Penal"? inmate, statute (law), maybe?

Clue "law" relates to statute, inmate (law enforcement) maybe income (law?). But "law" is not in lists. Could be.

Avoid opponent "ordinance" which is similar to statute (law). "law" might be ambiguous but that's okay because opponent has ordinance which is a law, but clue might cause guessing opponent's word? The partner might guess ordinance mistakenly. That may be risky. Might want clue that doesn't overlap.

Maybe clue "condiments"? only ketchup. Not many.

We want multiple target words. Let's try clue "money". Money relates to dime (coin), income (earnings). Could also relate to "statute" (money law?). Not.

Clue "tiny" relates to toothpick, dime, maybe egghead (small brain). Might be.

Clue "metal"? not.

Clue "prison"? inmate, maybe statute (law). Also might bring "concierge"? not.

Clue "sound"? jukebox, aircraft (engine sound) maybe.

Better to select clue "law" with targets: statute, inmate. But need to list them. That is okay. However "law" might get opponent "ordinance". But clue is not containing part of ordinance; but association could be ambiguous. But risk.

Better clue "coin" — relates to dime only. Not many.

Maybe choose clue "small" relating to toothpick, dime, egghead (small brain). Also maybe ketchup (small packet). Could be.

Let's decide: clue "legal". Targets: statute, inmate. That's two.

Check rule: clue cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them. "legal" is not any word. Good.

So final answer:

CLUE: legal
TARGETS: statute, inmate

Make sure formatting correct
